purposes.description: Solve combinatorial optimization problems (facility placement, knapsack, assignment, scheduling) by formulating variables/objectives/constraints, pruning search space, scoring candidates, and applying greedy + local search

primitives.evidence: Requires scipy.optimize (linprog, linear_sum_assignment, milp), numpy, and implementing DP, greedy, and local search algorithms
Optimization under constraints, scheduling, resource allocation with constraint propagation and multi-restart strategies
Multi-step scoring computations: marginal coverage gains, value/weight ratios, Manhattan/Euclidean distance calculations
Three-phase method (prune, score, anchor-based search) requires decomposing goal into ordered sub-steps with iterative refinement
Must follow the three-phase method in sequence: prune, score, then anchor-based search

alternative_paths.description: Dynamic programming path for exact 0/1 knapsack solutions when n < 10000

primitives.evidence: DP table construction and traceback using only standard Python (no third-party libs needed)
Multi-constraint satisfaction for DP state transitions
